desc:
The Greenway is surrounded on either side by picturesque hills and fields of
Middle-earth. It is wide and well-travelled. The road itself is composed of
smooth, well-worn stones. It stretches north and south, and is the primary route
for travel and commerce between Bree and Tharbad.
name: Greenway

desc:
The Greenway bends south and east here. It is wide and well-travelled, with a
well-worn track cut in the centre of the road. It is bordered on either side by
fields of waving grass and breathtaking views of the rolling hills and dense
forests that make up the heart of Middle-earth.
name: Greenway

desc:
Spanning north and south, the Greenway is wide and majestic. It is a handsome
road, lined on the bottom with heavy stones. The road makes travel through the
countryside very easy, and is capable of handling a lot of traffic. It leads
northward to the town of Bree, and southward to the shady town of Tharbad.
name: Greenway

desc:
The Greenway stretches north and south, flanked on its sides by lush, green
grasslands with beautiful vistas. The road is made of heavy stones, and is
well-worn in its centre, evidence of heavy travel. The road is the main
thoroughfare between Tharbad and Bree, and is used by travellers of all types.
name: Greenway

desc:
The winding Greenway cuts through the rolling grasslands, dotted with patches of
wildflowers and tall, waving grasses. Aside from deep wagon tracks, the road is
covered in lush grass. A few stunted trees and occasional bushes line the side
of the road.
name: Bend in the Greenway

desc:
The Greenway is surrounded by rolling fields of tall grass to the east and west,
with occasional clumps of bushes and small trees here and there. Aside from some
old wagon tracks pressed into the hard earth, the road doesn't seem to be used
very often.
name: Bend in the Greenway

desc:
Cutting through the rolling grasslands to the east and west, the Greenway snakes
its way across the green plains. Smaller bushes line the sides of the road,
leading into what seems to be a lush forest to the southwest and thicker brush
to the southeast.
name: Greenway

desc:
The Greenway makes its way closer to a forest of beech and birch to the west. To
the east, the northern grasslands gradually turn into brush-covered plains that
stretch out far to the southeast. A fallen, moss-covered signpost lies on the
side of the road.
name: Greenway

desc:
Thick brush to the east on one side, a sparse forest of beech and birch on the
other side to the west, the Greenway cuts a path through the vegetation. Far to
the north, rolling grasslands can be seen surrounding the road. A shallow, damp
ditch lines the western side of the road.
name: Greenway

desc:
The grass-covered Greenway skirts around the forested hill to the west, away
from the thick brush to the east. A fallen tree trunk lies halfway on the road
almost obstructing passage. A tall, jagged boulder stands on the opposite side
of the road, its weather-worn surface covered in lichen.
name: Greenway

desc:
The Greenway skirts around a forested hill to the northwest that overlooks this
section of the road and funnels the northern grasslands into a narrow pass
leading towards the southern lands. A high ledge rises up to the south and
separates the pass from the vast brushland to the southeast.
name: Bend in the Greenway

desc:
To the north, a forested hill sits high above a narrow pass, with the Greenway
coming in from the east and leading down towards the southern plains with high,
grass-covered ledges on both sides. The ridge to the southeast rises up from the
southeastern brushland like some sort of warning sign.
name: Bend in the Greenway

desc:
Below the ledges to either side, the Greenway winds through a narrow pass
between a forested hill above to the north and the southern grasslands. Bushes
and tufts of grass jut out from the walls of the pass, with a few boulders on
the side of the road, no doubt fallen from above.
name: Greenway

desc:
The Greenway leads from the greener plains to the south into a small, upward-
sloping pass that leads towards a forested hill far to the north. To the
northeast, a sharp ledge rises out in front of the endless brushland to the
southeast.
name: Greenway

desc:
Running between the tall grasses to the east and a thick brush to the west, the
Greenway makes its way from the small pass to the north into an eastward turn to
the southeast. Aside from some shallow wagon tracks, the road's surface is
completely covered in thick grass.
name: Greenway

desc:
The grass-covered Greenway runs past a thick brush to the west and turns
slightly east, farther away from the dark, foreboding thicket. To the north, the
road runs into a narrow upward-sloping pass towards a forested hillside. A thick
wall of tangled bushes lines the side of the road to the south.
name: Bend in the Greenway

desc:
The grass-covered dirt road runs past a thick, dark brush to the southwest,
turning north towards a narrow pass sloping upwards. Rolling grasslands stretch
out to the northeast, leading towards more brushlands in the distance.
name: Bend in the Greenway

desc:
A large, dusty road continues straight from the north to the south. Trees grow
densely to the west, perhaps a remnant of the ancient forest that used to cover
this land, but to the east it is predominantly dry grass with the occasional
bush.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road is packed hard from the passing horses, leaving an easy way to travel.
Trees grow on either side, but to the east of the road they rapidly thin out,
giving way to a plain. The road itself cuts a bold north-to-south route across
the countryside.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road is old and well-travelled, judging from the soil. Travelling on it is
quick and easy, but also a good place to be ambushed, with bushes closing in on
either side. The east side seems safe though, the bushes there being sparse
enough to see the plain beyond.
name: Greenway

desc:
Passage on this flat and hard road is easy, but the shrubs grow bushier and
denser on either side in the Tharbad direction. The road continues north and
east, skirting bramble bushes to the south. It might have been curved in this
direction by the builders due to a geographical feature. Whatever reason that is
can't be seen through the bushes.
name: Greenway

desc:
Turning from the west to the south, the roadway continues its long journey
between Bree and Tharbad. Though packed down to be as hard as rock, the dirt
which comprises the surface of the road nevertheless bears the marks of the
heavy use it has borne over the years since it was built. To the north, grassy
plains extend as far as the eye can see, while to the east there are numerous
bushes and shrubs.
name: Greenway

desc:
North and east, there is nothing to be seen except for a desolate plain, but
here there are numerous bushes and shrubs, including some bramble bushes. To the
west, there is a wide road skirting scrubland on the other side. To the south,
the bramble bushes become increasingly dense.
name: Bushes Bordering a Road

desc:
The plain here is dry and the ground hard. Small patches of short grass and the
occasional boulder are the only features in this barren place. The ground gently
rises over the plain towards the east. To the south the plain becomes more
lifeless and drier, while the north looks greener. Numerous bushes and shrubs
are visible to the west and south-west.
name: Short Grass

desc:
This land has been colonized by sea buckthorn and it seems to be doing
particularly well here, growing everywhere, swaying in the wind like waves in
the sea. These shrubs vary in height between three and sixteen feet tall
depending on maturity, with multiple tree-like branches. The bark is mostly dark
brown to black, though on younger shoots they have silvery hairs. The leaves are
shaped like lances with silvery hairs concealing their basic green colour. The
density of the shrubs means it is not possible to see very far in any direction.
name: Admist a Sea of Buckthorn

desc:
Blackthorn trees and other tall shrubs grow closely enough together here to
create a thick enough canopy to obscure the sky. The blackthorn has also formed
hedge-like barriers to the east, south and west, the dense spiny branches
preventing travel in those directions.
name: Amongst Blackthorn Trees

desc:
Here the fields are broken up by invading shrubs: mostly thorny bushes and
brambles, as the fields are surrounded by scrubland on three sides: west, where
the colourful common broom is invading from, north, where thick, thorny, shrubs
and bushes obstruct any travel, and east, where more bramble bushes are visible.
The broken fields continue to the south.
name: Broken Fields

desc:
Once a part of a forest, now only decayed tree stumps linger, breaking up the
fields. Bushes, predominantly clustered to the north-west, seem to be
encroaching here. Elsewhere, fields continue in all directions. A faint trace of
a trail or track is discernible leading eastward and to a dilapidated structure
to the west.
name: Broken Fields

desc:
This place is overrun with bramble bushes, a fact that makes getting anywhere
fast a bit difficult. The brambles have created particularly solid hedges around
the north and east of here, whereas to the south and west fields broken up by
invading shrubs seem like the obvious destination.
name: Amongst Brambles

desc:
Perhaps once part of a farm, these fields may have been used for grazing
livestock, though they seem to have been abandoned by the people that once lived
in this area. Brambles, more prevalent to the north, seem to be encroaching now
here too. There is the merest hint of a trail or track leading to the west and
the road to the east.
name: Abandoned Fields

desc:
This wide road travels inexorably onward, from north to south, through the flat
countryside. The ground is hard and marked by the evidence of the passage of
innumerable wagons over the years. To the west, a faint trail can be seen,
crossing a rough field. Another barren field lies to the east.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road narrows here as it passes through dense bushes. A large collection of
bushes and small trees can be seen just east of the road and a bramble hedge has
formed to the west making travel that way impossible. The road continues to the
north and south. To the north, a pile of stout timbers can be seen on the
ground.
name: Greenway

desc:
The wide road passes into a dense part of the scrubland here, with tall trees,
bushes and shrubs closing off the west side, and dense high bramble bushes
closing off the east side. Some stout timbers lie on the ground to the north and
south.
name: Greenway

desc:
In a small clearing in the scrub, crude shelters have been made to provide
protection from the elements. Many bedrolls and a fireplace seem to indicate
that this is more or less a permanent camp. A hole just outside the camp,
obviously a latrine, lends more weight to this theory.
name: Robber's Camp

desc:
These fields must once have been a part of a forest. Now only rotten roots and
trunks, hidden among a few stunted trees and small shrubs remain. To the south,
the scene is even more bleak, as the landscape opens out into a vast,
featureless plain. To the north, shrubs have grown up.
name: Broken Fields

desc:
Hardy sea buckthorn grows here near the edge of this expanse of scrubland, the
shrubs becoming smaller in the southerly direction, hinting at a gradual
invasion of the plains beyond. Towards the north, there are more numerous
blackberry bushes and blackthorn bushes.
name: Near the Edge of an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
Thornbushes are the dominant plant species of note here, with various types
doing well including blackberry bushes and blackthorn. A mix of these provides a
barrier to the north. Some sea buckthorn grows to the south, but elsewhere there
is little change to the flora.
name: Thorny Scrubland

desc:
A mass of brambles lies to the north and runs towards the west where it meets
other thorny shrubs. It is both far too high and too deep to jump and too dense
to push through. With the right equipment, someone might be able to cut a way
through, but it would take hours and the brambles seem to diminish towards the
east anyway. Elsewhere, smaller bramble bushes and other bushes and low plants
dot the landscape.
name: Near a Mass of Brambles

desc:
This place appears to be a dell or hollow and, emerging from the northern slope,
where it was taking an underground route, a small stream of water makes a few
small pools as it flows gently east. As it reaches the eastern slope, it forms a
larger pool, but it is not clear where, if anywhere, the water goes next.
Growing on the eastern slope, blackthorn, with its dense spiny branches, has
formed a hedge-like barrier. There are many bushes in all directions and
particularly dense undergrowth to the north.
name: Dell amongst the Bushes

desc:
The almost continuous shade of the tall shrubs here does not seem to be
preventing smaller evergreen bushes from growing. There is a particularly dense
tangle of these to the south. To the north and west, the dark scrubland
apparently continues, but the east seems brighter.
name: Amongst Bushes and Tall Shrubs

desc:
There are many different types of shrubs here, but the common broom is
particularly noticeable for its prevalence, substantial size, and, in spring and
summer, the profuse golden yellow flowers that cover it. To the north, west, and
east the land is covered with similar-sized bushes and shrubby plants, but to
the south the land opens up with little growing.
name: Amongst Common Broom

desc:
These fields must have once been a part of a forest. Now, however, the land is
littered with the decaying remains of tree stumps, which lie amid a few sickly
bushes and patches of yellowing grass. To the north, the bushes look healthier
and more prevalent.
name: Broken Fields

desc:
These fields must have once been a part of a great forest as rotten tree stumps
are all over the place. Beyond a decaying building, plains stretch out to the
south and more fields can be seen to the east and the west. To the north, young
trees and bushes are visible.
name: Broken Fields

desc:
The terrain is flat, and covered in a thick, green carpet of grass stretching
out what appears to be indefinitely to the south. A small farm can be seen in
the distance to the east, and the low bellows of cows mooing can be heard on
occasion.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
A flat, calm sea of thick green grasses stretches out to the north and south.
Small animals and critters appear to have taken up residence here and there,
with small mounds and dens dotting the landscape. Small patches of rocks and
gravel line the ground here and there.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The flat, green terrain stretches as far as the eye can see in every direction.
Small patches of thin shrubs and trees appear occasionally, breaking up the
otherwise monotonous landscape. The ground is firm and densely packed, allowing
easy passage in any direction here.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The lush grassland stretches out in all directions, sloping slightly upwards to
the east. An old, nigh-invisible footpath leads to the south, snaking between a
few boulders and some groups of bushes. What might have once been a scarecrow
now lies in a rotting pile of wood and rope on the ground, almost completely
covered in grass.
name: Lush Grassland

desc:
A faded footpath snakes through the grassy fields. A few ancient wooden
fenceposts jut out from the grass-covered earth, indicating that this area might
have once been farmland. A bigger group of trees and bushes can be seen in the
distance to the south.
name: Footpath among the Grassy Fields

desc:
A group of larger bushes and small trees stands out from among the vast
grasslands, hiding the burnt and dilapidated remains of a larger building. A
broken stone wall still stands to the south, almost completely covered in vines
and grass. Among the stone rubble, a partly burnt section of a wooden floor
still remains, grass growing from between the floorboards.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
A large farm can be seen a ways off in the distance to the west. Everywhere
else, a lush, green field spawls out in all directions. The occasional sound of
farm animals can be heard in the distance. Hills, mounds, and small holes have
been made by small rodents or other animals, with small game trails worn into
the grass.
name: Field near a Farm

desc:
This is a small garden, with lots of flowers and green plants. The plants are
placed in lines, as if someone put them there on purpose. The soil smells like
manure, but this seems to be a great place to grow plants. A farmhouse and a
barn stand nearby.
name: Small Garden

desc:
Crumbling ruins stand in the centre of the field here dominating the immediate
landscape. Large stone blocks lie stacked in rows, covered in lichen and moss,
forming the perimeter of what must have once been a very large structure. Tall
grass and weeds grow all around the blocks, almost reclaiming them back for the
land.
name: Crumbling Ruins

desc:
Though the terrain is relatively flat, an occasional low hill rises from the
ground and rolls back down. Green grass stretches out as far as the eye can see
in every direction. Small footpaths can be seem worn into the ground, probably
from some small game. A gentle breeze blow through the air.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grassy field is a serene and peaceful place, a haven of tranquility and
calm. The tall blades of grass wave gently in the breeze. Wildflowers add a
touch of colour to the landscape, their delicate petals unfurling to soak up the
sun's rays. The field is alive with the sound of rustling grass and the soft
buzz of insects, and it seems to go on forever.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The grass here is tall and lush, and it stretches out endlessly in all
directions, seeming to go on forever. Here and there, patches of thick,
waist-high weeds can be found, adding splashes of colour to the landscape. Thin
lines of small trees dot the plains, their branches reaching up toward the sky.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
This stretch of woods is not nearly as ancient as the stand of ancient oaks
farther south. Sticking out of the ground are the remnants of the foundation of
an old stone building. The light undergrowth almost obscures the stones, but
this area was definitely inhabited by man at some point in the past.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
The fertile plain stretches out, the rich soil yielding an abundance of crops
and vegetation. The landscape is dotted with fields of grain, groves of fruit
trees, and gardens bursting with colourful flowers. The air is filled with the
sound of chirping birds and the soft rustle of leaves in the breeze.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
A grassy field stretches out in all directions, the tall blades of green waving
gently in the breeze. Wildflowers dot the landscape, adding splashes of colour
to the verdant expanse. The air is filled with the scent of growth and life, and
the sounds of birds and insects can be heard.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The landscape here is dry and vast, parched and sunbaked. The grass is yellowed
and withered, struggling to survive in the harsh conditions. The air is hot and
stale,and the ground is cracked and dry. Small clouds of dust are kicked after
each footstep.
name: Dry Grassland

desc:
The dry grasslands present a barren and unforgiving landscape, the parched earth
and withered grass a testament to the harsh conditions. The air is hot and dry,
and the sun beats down mercilessly upon the earth. The grass struggles to
survive in this environment, its blades yellowed and wilted.
name: Desolate Field

desc:
The ground is dry and covered in short grass with patches of bare dirt spread
throughout. There are some rocks and stones scattered about the landscape,
adding to the bare ground. Thorny, scrubby bushes make an appearance every now
and then.
name: Bleak Grassland

desc:
Lush green grass covers the terrain in all directions. To the southwest, the
ground slopes downwards like a huge, green stairway. Small bushes and an
occasional little hill give a slight sense of depth in the encompassing
greenery.
name: Field of Grass

desc:
Wide rolling fields of tall grass stretch out in all directions with only an
old, grass-covered dirt road cutting through them to the east. To the southwest,
the grassland slopes downwards as if some huge steps were carved into the earth
ages ago.
name: Rolling Grass

desc:
Between the grass-covered Greenway to the east and a small dip to the west, the
vast grassland dominates the area around here. In the distance to the south, the
tall grass meets a small forest hugging the side of the road.
name: Grassland by the Greenway

desc:
A sparse forest of mostly beech and birch trees stands tall on the side of the
Greenway to the east, stretching farther southwest. The vast green grasslands of
the northern plains end abruptly upon meeting the northernmost trees of the
forest here.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
The lush green grasslands continue in all directions, with only a slight upward
slope leading east towards the Greenway. A few bushes and smaller trees dot the
landscape, with an occasional boulder or two jutting out from the grass-covered
ground.
name: Grassy Slope

desc:
The terrain here is flat and green, teeming with wildlife. The buzzing of
insects, chirping of birds, and the occasional distant sound of farm animals
fill the air. There are sporadic stands of small, thinning trees, as well as
small patches of high weeds and grasses.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
This section of grassland runs between two steeper ledges to the east and west,
sloping slightly upwards to the north to meet the rolling plains there. Farther
south, the tall grass gives way to clumps of bushes and smaller trees, heralding
the forest far to the south.
name: Tall Grass below a Ledge

desc:
A flat section of lush grassland widens to the north, running between some
steeper ledges to the east and west. To the south, the tall grasses give way to
a sparse forest of beech and birch. Below the eastern ledge, the weathered
remains of a crashed wagon lie in a heap, most likely fallen or pushed down from
the forested edge above.
name: Ruined Wagon below a Ledge

desc:
The sparse forest of beech and birch meets a steep ledge overlooking the grassy
plains below to the west. A section of flat grassland runs northward from the
forest's edge. Among the undergrowth, only a few larger bushes and the
occasional boulder give some cover.
name: Forested Ledge

desc:
Slim beeches and birch trees stand tall, with grass and sparse bushes dotting
the undergrowth, mixed with a few smaller boulders. The grassy Greenway runs
along the eastern edge of the forest, a shallow ditch separating the two.
name: Roadside Forest

desc:
Slim birches and beeches stand tall, overlooking the grasslands below to the
west. The Greenway snakes closer to the southeast, passing the forest and
turning southward into a shallow pass running between the surrounding
grasslands.
name: Woody Plateau

desc:
This is the highest point of the surrounding forest, with tall trees of beech
and birch overlooking the Greenway that skirts the forest's southeastern corner
before turning south into a pass. On the other side of the road, thick brushland
can be seen stretching out into the southeast.
name: Forested Hill above the Road

desc:
A narrow plateau connects the lower plains to the beech forest to the north,
covered with thick grass and clumps of bushes. Below and to the east, the
Greenway passes through a narrow pass. Below the ledge to the west, some
grass-capped, dome-shaped hills rise out from the ground.
name: Grassy Plateau above the Greenway

desc:
As the Greenway snakes its way through the eastern pass and meets the southern
plains, this little grass-covered ledge sits above the pass, providing a good
vantage point to spot movement below. To the west, a narrow ridge stretches out
and up, separating the grassy plains to the northwest from the thick brush to
the southwest.
name: Grassy Overlook above the Greenway

desc:
To the east, the Greenway heads into a narrow pass that sits between the
southern brushland and the grassy plains to the north. To the south and
southwest, the green grass gradually turns into a thick and impenetrable brush.
name: Grasses by the Greenway

desc:
Dense bushes have overtaken the grasslands completely around here, growing
denser and denser farther west. To the east, the Greenway comes out of a pass to
the north and makes its way past the brush, slightly turning east towards the
grassy plains there.
name: Bushes by the Greenway

desc:
Dense bushes and tangled undergrowth cover the uneven ground here and climb up a
steep and dangerous-looking slope to the west, into even thicker brush. The
thicket follows the Greenway like a shadow looming over the snaking,
grass-covered road.
name: Roadside Thicket

desc:
This grass-covered, narrow ridge rises up above the round hills that lie far
below to the west. Beyond the hills, a stretch of thick brushland covers most of
the ground to the southwest. The ground here slopes downward to the southeast
towards the Greenway that makes its way through a pass there.
name: Grassy Ridge

desc:
Endless green grass stretches out in all directions, with only some steeper
ledges above to the east and below to the west, dividing the lush greenery into
narrow sections. Aside from the occasional bush or small tree, only a small
clump of trees can be seen far below in the distance to the southwest.
name: Narrow, Grassy Ledge

desc:
The rolling grasslands undulate eastward like huge natural steps, with bushes
and a few smaller trees jutting out from the higher points. A small footpath
runs parallel to the nearest ledge, disappearing into the tall grass to the
north.
name: Grasslands along a Ledge

desc:
An old stone staircase leads down into a mostly intact cellar. The uneven stone
floor is littered with shards of broken glass and pieces of rotten crates and
shelves. A few crooked wine racks are still upright, covered with a thick layer
of dust and cobwebs. Large reddish-brown stains can be seen among the broken
glass.
name: Small Wine Cellar

desc:
Up above to the east, a forest grows tall on a high ledge, overlooking the
surrounding grassy plains. To the south, a large pile of boulders seems to have
spilt into the grassy fields from above. A larger clump of trees and bushes
stands out from the otherwise grassy plains to the northwest.
name: Grassy Field along a Forested Ledge

desc:
The rolling grasslands stop abruptly at an overgrown stone wall to the north,
the only standing remnants of some sort of building. Thick bushes and a few
trees almost completely hide it from sight. To the south, a few small hills
stand out from among the grassy fields.
name: Overgrown Stone Wall

desc:
The rolling plains of grass stretch out in all directions, with a few bushes and
trees standing out from the overwhelmingly green sea. To the southeast, a few
small hills can be seen jutting out from the grassland, sprinkled with piles of
boulders and scraggly bushes.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
This pocket of lush grassland is surrounded by a forested ledge to the northeast
and some small boulder-covered hills to the southwest. Aside from a large pile
of fallen boulders to the north, the flat grasslands stretch out far to the
north.
name: Green Corner below a Ledge

desc:
The dome-shaped hills are almost completely covered with grass and bushes, with
a few smaller boulders mixed in as well. Near the ground level, however, pieces
of ancient stonework can be glimpsed from beneath the grass, revealing that the
hills haven't formed naturally but have rather been built.
name: Grassy Mounds

desc:
Springing out of the ground, these small hills look like oversized mushroom
caps, round and jolly. Beneath the grass-covered exterior, the hills hide some
ancient stonework, however. Instead of natural formations, the hills are in
actuality outer domes of some ancient underground structures.
name: Grassy Domes

desc:
The thick and dark brushland to the south comes to an end as the ground here
slopes upward a bit. Dome-shaped hills rise out from the ground, covered in
green grass and bushes. To the east, a high, grass-covered ledge angles upward
to look over the hills like an oversized sundial.
name: Fields below a Grass-Covered Ledge

desc:
The grassy hills to the north are engulfed by the thick brush here, tangled
branches almost completely obscuring the sky. Damp, moss-covered undergrowth
consists of thorny vines and hanging moss, making traversing almost like
navigating a natural maze.
name: Thick Brush

desc:
Right below the grass-covered ridge to the north, thick bushes and stunted trees
reach up towards the sky. The ground here is moist and springy, with thick
strands of hanging moss and climbing vines making it difficult to tell trees
apart as it all blends into one, continuous thicket.
name: Moist Thicket

desc:
Branches and vines are covered with thick strands of hanging moss around here,
obscuring vision. To the east, the vines seem to be less restricting, the
Greenway barely visible beyond. The uneven, moss-covered ground slightly slopes
upwards to the southwest, leading farther into the brush.
name: Mossy Thicket

desc:
The ground slopes down into a damp depression here, with scraggly branches and
hanging vines surrounding a small pool of stagnant water below a steep rocky
rise to the south. The stones scattered around the edge of the pool are covered
with a slick green substance.
name: Stagnant Pool

desc:
The tangled branches and vines give way to smaller, stunted bushes and less
dense undergrowth to the east. The ground slopes downward towards the barely
visible Greenway in the distance, branches and roots jutting out from the ground
and making the descent quite precarious.
name: Tangled Rise

desc:
Strands of hanging moss and climbing vines make this part of the brush extremely
hard to navigate. A jagged boulder juts out from the ground to the north,
standing above a little stagnant pool. To the east, a once dried-out brook is
now a completely overgrown ditch leading towards the road far to the east.
name: Tangled Vines

desc:
Tall shrubs overshadow this location. Not much grows at ground level, though
whether this is the impact of the foliage canopy or whether browsers keep new
growth at bay is hard to tell. The scrubland continues in all directions, but it
feels like there is something of a change to the south.
name: Amongst Tall Shrubs

desc:
The shade of elder trees and the mossy ground help to keep the soil damp, but
this area may also be liable to occasional floods as it looks like there may be
a stream to the south in a deep dell. To the north tall shrubs are visible at
the skyline, while to the east the scrubland also continues.
name: Amongst Elder Trees

desc:
This deep dell is secluded, with short grass growing along much of the bottom
and various shrubs growing upwards towards the open sky. A line of mostly smooth
stones hints at where a seasonal stream may live out its dying days. The dell
continues to the east and west, while the sides of the dell to the north and
south are covered with slippery green moss.
name: In a Deep Dell

desc:
Tall hawthorn trees stand all around, blocking almost any view of the sky above,
and making passage through this scrubland tiresome. Dense and spiny blackthorn
blocks any passage or sight to the east, while the scrubland continues in the
other directions.
name: Amongst Hawthorn Trees

desc:
Shrubs, tall and bushy, shield the ground from the sun's penetrating rays.
Brambles grow thick and tall to the south as they try and reach the light. The
dark scrubland continues to the east and north, but to the west it looks
lighter.
name: Amongst Brambles and Tall Shrubs

desc:
Thorny shrubs prevail in this area of scrubland, their thorns designed to
provide some protection for their leaves from browsing animals. This does not
appear to have been particularly effective, as few leaves near the ground are
visible. The scrubland seems to continue to the east, south and presumably to
the north, although visibility in any direction is poor.
name: Thorny Scrubland

desc:
Here tall blackthorn trees, spiny and densely branched, create a canopy of
foliage blocking out the sky above and making travel in any direction difficult.
The dense scrubland also seems to continue in every direction, though it's
impossible to see very far.
name: Amongst Blackthorn Trees

desc:
This place is shrouded from any view of the sky above by the dense, dark foliage
of tall evergreen shrubs of various kinds. The scrubland probably continues in
all directions, though not much can be seen to the east over high and thickly
growing brambles, and densely growing blackthorn trees block travel southwards
too.
name: Under a Canopy of Tall Shrubs

desc:
The landscape here is dominated by mature shrubs, mainly blackthorn and
hawthorn, tall and leafy enough and closely packed enough to create effective
shade from any sun that may beat down upon the ground from the sky. These thorny
shrubs also make travel tiresome as it is impossible to move in a straight line
for long, or indeed to see very far.
name: Amongst Blackthorn and Hawthorn

desc:
Tall, leafy and mostly thorny, shrubs stand all around in this scrubland.
Looking to the south and west, more of the same, as far as it is possible to see
through the bushes and under the canopy of branches, but to the north dense and
spiny blackthorn creates an effective barrier: it could almost be a planted
hedge. There is a sense of the tall shrubs perhaps dying out towards the east,
but this may be an illusion.
name: Beneath Tall Shrubs

desc:
Young trees and saplings have grown up in this area, probably spreading from the
north where a remnant of the ancient forest remains. Bushes compete for space
with the young trees and in some cases, it looks like the saplings grew up
through the bushes and will eventually shade them. The scrubland continues to
the west and south, but to the east a road is visible.
name: Amongst Young Trees

desc:
The tall trees of this light forest are spread well apart, making passage across
the firm, loamy soil quite easy, and lending an open, airy feeling to the place.
To the east, a wide road can be seen, skirting the woodland, whilst westward,
the shrubby trees close in somewhat, shading the ground from the sky above.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A canopy of tall evergreen shrubs creates an obscuring veil, hiding any view of
the sky above. The scrubland likely extends in all directions; however, vision
is limited due to all the shrubs and brambles, the latter of which also block
the way west.
name: Under a Canopy of Tall Shrubs

desc:
Various types of shrubs populate this area: blackthorn dominates the northern
landscape, hindering passage in that direction; sea buckthorn prevails in the
southern expanse; hawthorn is more abundant towards the west, alongside other
smaller shrubs scattered throughout. An unobstructed view to the east reveals
the presence of a visible road.
name: Scrubland near a Road

desc:
Short, dry grass here sways gently in the breeze. Thick, prickly brambles can be
seen to the north-east, extending in a sprawling mass as far as the eye can see
in that direction. The view stretches out for miles in other directions over the
grass, with nothing of significance to be seen.
name: Short Grass near Some Brambles

desc:
Small holes in the ground punctuate the grass here, but it is the wall or mound
of brambles to the north that are most striking. It would be impossible to get
through those mature and overgrown brambles, but the leaves, flowers and
blackberries probably keep a lot of animals happy. To the west, the brambles die
out, while the grass continues, also to the south.
name: South of a Bramble Hedge

desc:
Dense bramble bushes have taken hold in this area, particularly to the west
where their density and height mean nothing is visible on the other side of
them. To the north, the brambles become increasingly sparse, and some other
bushes and shrubs are making a living. To the east, the land seems to noticeably
open up, while to the south there is a very deep gully.
name: Amongst the Brambles

desc:
The grass is almost non-existent on this desolate plain. The soil is parched and
thin. A river must once have passed just south of here, for a wide and deep
gully cuts into the earth, making a scar that runs from east to west across the
land. Whilst travel to the south would be made difficult by this feature, it
seems that progress in that direction might still be possible. The plain
continues to the north and east, but to the west bushes and shrubs are growing.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
A little thin and unhealthy grass clings to life here, on this parched plain. To
the south, a rather deep gully runs from east to west, perhaps the bed of some
ancient river, now long ago dried up and forgotten. The plain stretches onward
north, east and west from here, with perhaps some scrubland beyond to the west.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
A dry plain is all that can be seen in the west, whilst to the north the horizon
is dominated by an apparent line of vegetation of some kind. The ground here
seems parched and quite unable to sustain life, as indicated by the thin wisps
of unhealthy grass that seem barely able to cling to life. To the south, a gully
cuts a deep scar across the countryside.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
A river must once have cut this mighty scar in the countryside, but now it
stands quite dry. Though shallow here, it deepens markedly to the west, and the
way eastward is blocked by a huge pile of fallen boulders. The dry plain
continues to the north and south.
name: Shallow Gully

desc:
The plain here is rather dry and dead. A few patches of grass are all the life
to be found. This plain could once have been flourishing with life but must have
died away when the river to the north-west dried out. The dry plain continues to
the north, south and west.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
This is quite a deep gully here, but it gets shallower to the east and continues
to deepen to the west. It is impossible to see out beyond the slopes, where some
hardy bushes cling to life amongst the stones and dry, cracked mud. A river must
have made this deep gully centuries ago and any torrential rainfall surely comes
this way.
name: Deepening Gully

desc:
The river, which once cut this mighty channel through the land, may have gone
south from here through a dark gulch. Here the gully is deep, becoming shallower
to the west and east, and narrower and steeper also to the west. Dry, cracked
mud hints at occasional water on the surface and some bushes growing here must
have found it beneath.
name: Deep Gully

desc:
The gully becomes narrower and steeper here, and shallower to the west, where it
seems to reach a dead end in a mass of vegetation. To the east, it becomes quite
deep and wide as it cuts across the countryside. To the north and south, the
smooth rock walls are not suitable for climbing up out of the gully.
name: End of a Gully

desc:
This spur of land stands between two deep scars in the dry plains, all that now
appears to remain of an old river. Long ago this formed a gully to the north and
a narrower and even deeper gulch to the west and south. The dry plain continues
to the east.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The gulch cleaves a north-to-south gash through the landscape, its towering
walls curving towards each other, creating a tunnel-like passage that conceals
the sky from below. The ground underfoot is cracked, and some plants have taken
root, struggling to thrive in the dim light. The walls of the gulch are of cold,
smooth stone.
name: Dark Gulch

desc:
The muddy walls close in here, giving room for only three to walk abreast. The
floor is squishy, with water seeping in from all over the walls and trickling
down to the floor in a constant, dripping flow. The passage widens to the east
and west.
name: Wet and Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The muddy tunnel bends sharply here, with the walls banked at an angle that
makes footing precarious. A rough path has been trod into the bank, though only
a single handspan wide. A faint trickle of water runs along the inside edge,
with a low, constant burble.
name: Bend in the Wet Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel slopes sharply here as it curves down into the depths. The banked
walls seem to have been formed by a massive flow of water. A few pockets line
the muddy walls, appparently the home to some subterranean creatures. The sound
of flowing water can be heard from below.
name: Sloping Passageway

desc:
An underground river flows through the dank tunnel here. The source is hidden,
apparently stemming from beneath the rocks. The river continues to the east,
while the shoreline of the tunnel can be found to the north. Brown, mud-slick
walls prevent passage in any other direction.
name: Underground River

desc:
The air is heavy here with the smell of wet dirt and cave fungus. To the south,
the tunnel floor gives way to the shoreline of an underground river. The water
is moving, though only a small ripple can be heard. To the west, the tunnel
continues on.
name: Dank Tunnel

desc:
The floor here is wet and squishy, with a coating of slick mud covering every
visible surface. The faint burble of rushing water can be heard to the north,
washing out most other sounds. The tunnel continues to the west, with a few
boreholes marring the walls, evidently the homes of some small creatures. A dirt
ramp leads down into the darkness.
name: Deep, Dank Tunnel

desc:
The ceiling hangs low over this underground river, leaving little space to
breathe. The current is not very strong, but it is steady, and it seems to pull
down below the walls. The babble of the river is a faint noise in the background
of cave.
name: Underground River

desc:
This river is deep, with the bottom far out of reach. The walls curve into the
ceiling, and are covered with a thick layer of grime. A steady current flows,
though neither its source nor destination are apparent. To the east, the shore
rises up out of the water.
name: Underground River

desc:
This ledge is narrow and sloped, with its floor wet with the overflow of the
underground river. Short burrows line the walls, apparently the home of some
reptilian beasts. The ledge continues to the east, while it descends into the
river to the west.
name: Ledge along an Underground River

desc:
A dirt ramp spirals around the walls of this vertical tunnel. Off the ramp, the
bottom of the tunnel can be seen far below. A few groves are carved into the
centre of the ramp, indicating where something has been dragged up and down. A
couple of brass lanterns hang from hooks in the wall.
name: Dirt-carved Ramp

desc:
A ramp of smooth dirt and mud curves up around the tunnel walls here. A few
lines of dirt mark the centre of the ramp, perhaps caused by dragging some
sledge through the damp floor. A few torches line the walls, bathing the room in
shadows.
name: Base of a Muddy Ramp

desc:
This curving passage has been hacked through the clay and earth, with pick marks
scarring every wall. A trodden path leads from north to east, forming a slight
trench through the muddy ground. Crude iron sconces line the walls, each
flickering with a small flame.
name: Hewn Tunnel-bend

desc:
The damp tunnel turns here, and the ceiling drops abruptly with the bend. A few
pools of water dot the floor, each with a faint gurgle indicating they are not
stagnant. But the source of the water is not immediately visible. The tunnel
continues to the east and south.
name: Wet Tunnel Bend

desc:
The water stops abruptly here as it reaches the tunnel shore to the north. The
undercurrent seems to continue beyond, though exactly where is unclear. A few
notches in the wall demonstrate that something, or someone, has disturbed the
mud.
name: Abrupt End of the River

desc:
The muddy passageway leads into the black depths of the underground river here,
leaving a only a wet route to the south. To the west, the mud continues into the
darkness. It is unclear where the water leads, as the current seems to flow
below the surface and into the walls.
name: Shore of the Underground River

desc:
At the western end of the passage, the ground drops abruptly into a slick, muddy
ramp. A few trickles of water flow doing the ramp, though not nearly enough to
have formed the passage. The tunnel continues to the east, while a dark hollow
has been carved in the northern wall.
name: Above a Steep Drop

desc:
The dank tunnel comes to a wet end here, with mud and grime the main
distinguishing feature. A few bulbous stalactites hang from the ceiling, but
they look less than stable as evidenced by the broken pile of white rock
covering the floor.
name: End of a Wet Tunnel

desc:
The walls of this tunnel are covered in bulges and pits that show the excavators
cared little for making clearn lines. A small lantern in a hollow of the eastern
wall casts a wan light over the chamber. The tunnel continues to the east, while
a thick door blocks passage to the south.
name: Rough-cut Tunnel

desc:
A roughly fashioned stairwell of earth and packed rock has been built here. The
steps curve in a spiral up the wall, supported by thin beams of timber. This
cavern is wide, and appears a mix of natural cavities and excavated earth. A
pair of lanterns hang along one wall, lighting the chamber.
name: Base of an Earthen Stairway

desc:
The earthen stairwell winds its way around this dark excavation. Wooden rafters
support the structure from below, though they seem barely sufficient to
withstand the load. Torches have been stuff into pockets in the walls, casting
flickering shadows throughout the chamber.
name: Narrow, Winding Stairway

desc:
A narrow staircase of packed earth has been carved into the wall of this
vertical tunnel. There is no railing, and the steep drop-off at the inside edge
of the steps seems dangerous. At the top, the stairs lead right into a wooden
hatch set in the earthen ceiling. A lantern hangs near the hatch, lightning the
tunnel below.
name: Steep Staircase of Packed Earth

desc:
Water drips from the ceiling of this dingy storage room. Crates and shelves line
the walls, though most appear to be filled with rubbish. The storage room
continues to the south, and a door fashioned from planks provides egress to the
east.
name: Dingy Storage Room

desc:
The tunnel slopes steeply here as the muddy passage winds upward. Deep grooves
are carved into the curved walls, apparently caused by some massive rush of
water. At the base of the slope, the burrow turns to the south as it stretches
into the darkness.
name: Slope in the Tunnel

desc:
Brown, muddy water streams from the ceiling in a constant pitter patter of tiny
droplets. Stalactites have been begun to form overhead, while small pools have
rest below the flows, dotting the floor like shiny speckles. The dank tunnel
continues its muddy path to the north and south.
name: Dripping Tunnel

desc:
The air here is thick with moisture with the faint scent of earth and mildew
hanging heavily. Water droplets cling to the muddy walls, dripping rhythmically
down to the wet floor. The dark passage continues its muddy way to the north and
south.
name: Damp and Dark Passage

desc:
The tunnel twists at a sharp angle, the walls growing wetter and more uneven as
it bends out of sight. A low, steady drip echoes through the air, mingling with
the trickles of water that cross the uneven floor. The passage seems to have
been carved from some great rush of water, though that seems to have been some
time ago.
name: Wet Curving Passage

desc:
The tunnel curves sharply and the winding floor is caked in thick, sucking mud.
The walls, slick with moisture, are streaked with dark, wet marks where they
have been disturbed by some unknown creature. The air is heavy with the the
scent of mud and grime, and every possible path seems to be swallowed quickly by
the oppressive darkness.
name: Muddy Tunnel-bend

desc:
The tunnel turns here, leading off to the north and east. The ground is moist
and soft with small trickles of water pushing up through the mud. The walls and
ceiling are pockmarked with deep indentations, apparently made by some burrowing
creature.
name: Winding Tunnel

desc:
The muddy tunnel slopes upward here in a banked, twisting ramp. To the west, the
passage widens as it spits off to the north and south. The walls are smooth, as
if carved from the very waters of the earth. A faint trickle of water flows down
here, heading to the west.
name: Steep Muddy Tunnel

desc:
This water of this murky pool churns in a spiral of brown and red muck. It seems
to be deep, with no bottom visible from the surface. The shoreline of the muddy
tunnel lies to the east, while wet walls of dirt and clay surround the rest of
the pool.
name: Swirling Pool

desc:
Umber-coloured water is all that can be seen in every direction. A soft current
traverses this passage way, though neither the source nor the destination can be
discerned. Even the exit is difficult to make out, though the water seems to
come to a surface far above.
name: Submerged Passage

desc:
This muddy passage is narrow, with the muck-covered walls closing in from all
sides save the south. Dark circles dot the floor of this underground river,
apparently the nesting ground for some fish or amphibian. The water has a
current to it, though the source is difficult to pinpoint.
name: Submerged Passage

desc:
The brown water of this pool swirls in a muddy torrent of dangerous energy. The
walls and ceiling covered in a thick layer of dark, wet earth, dotted with
pockets that seem to connect below the surface. The pool continues to the north,
while the walls close in from every other direction.
name: Pool of Swirling Water

desc:
Water swirls around this cavern in a powerful current of darkness. The walls are
thick with mud. Deep, black holes spot the muddy walls in a speckled pattern.
The holes seem to connect, perhaps as a home for some gigantic, burrowing
creature. The brown water of the pool is entirely opaque, hiding what lies
beneath the surface.
name: Dank and Cavernous Pool

desc:
This hollow in the side of the tunnel is relatively dry, setting it apart from
dankness around. A depression at one end of the the tunnel has been covered in
leaves and small branches, evidently the home of some cave-dwelling creature. A
few deep marks have been gouged into the walls, apparently made by some sharp
claws.
name: Dark Hollow

desc:
This place seems to be near the edge of an expanse of scrubland, extending as it
is from here to the north and west, but with broken fields to the south and
east. A variety of shrubs grow here, most noticeably common broom is encroaching
from the west, but there are also some small trees and saplings suggesting that
this area may have been a forest that was cleared some years ago.
name: Near the Edge of an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
This dry and barren field comes to an abrupt end to the east where it meets the
rim of a gulch cutting a scar across the landscape. The walls of this chasm are
almost vertical, and comprised of smooth, dark rock, impossible to climb safely.
Apparently, this was once the course of a substantial waterway, which has long
ago dried up and been forgotten. The steep, narrow gully that leads to the gulch
is to the north. To the west, a wide road makes its way south through the plain,
which continues to the south.
name: Barren Field near a Road

desc:
Short grass covers the terrain, rocking in the wind. In the distance to the east
and south-east, some brambles add texture to the landscape. There are wide-open
fields to the south and west as far as the eye can see. To the north and
north-east, there's the hint of the odd bush or shrub
name: Short Grass near Some Brambles

desc:
Long grass here could provide cover for a variety of small animals. There are
also some bramble bushes, but these seem to form a massive wall or hedge to the
south, and nothing can be seen beyond them. To the west, the long grass
continues, while to the north lie small bushes and shrubs.
name: North of a Bramble Hedge

desc:
Short grass, interspersed with green moss, covers the ground here. In all
directions, an uninterrupted view is offered for miles around as the land here
is nearly flat and there isn't much else growing. West and south, the grassy
lowland plains flank a wide road, running from north to south, snaking through
the countryside. The plains also continue to the north and east.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The grass within this vicinity has nearly perished entirely. What scant patches
endure are fragmented into small clusters, displaying a sickly, malnourished
demeanour. These barren fields find themselves practically surrounded by a dry
plain, albeit with scrubland visible to the south and enclosing a roadway to the
south-west.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
The tall grasses of the plains to the north fade here as the land becomes less
fertile, and the ground hardens. There is scrubland to the west and south and a
wide road can be seen cutting a path through the bushes and trees. To the east,
the plains stretch onward, rising slightly.
name: Plain beside the Greenway

desc:
It is clear that this is the boundary of a larger area of scrubland: a variety
of small bushes and shrubs dot this landscape, with the occasional bramble bush
too, but to the north the bushes and shrubs are more developed, with less grass
visible between them. However, grass predominates to the west and south.
name: Edge of an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
The scrubland to the north and east thins down to an occasional bush here. Short
grass dominates this almost barren grassland. The reason for the scarcity of
vegetation is perhaps the dryness of the ground, which makes it difficult for
plants to grow here. To the south-east one can see some brambles, but the land
is quite featureless to the south and west.
name: Near an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
The scrubland to the north has diminished to the point where there are just a
few shrubs and bushes amongst the grassy plain here. The land continues
similarly to the west, south and east, with short dry grass predominating.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Short dry grass and moss cover the ground here. A few hardy bushes are trying to
colonize this area, but the wind and poor soil keep out most of the potential
invaders. The ground falls away slightly to the west too, perhaps draining heavy
rainfall that way. Tall bushes are visible to the north, but elsewhere the
grassy plain continues.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
A flat, almost featureless grassy plain parallels the Greenway, as it travels
south through the countryside. Here and there, an occasional bush or flimsy
sapling stand, bent in the wind. To the east, the plains rise slightly, but
north and south, the endless grassland stretches out into the distance.
name: Grassland beside the Greenway

desc:
Evergreen shrubs grow densely in this area, creating thick and intricate
vegetation that tree saplings have a hard time breaking through. The air is
scented, filled with the smell of the plants. Tall thorn bushes and new-growth
trees are discernible to the north, while to the west and south the land appears
more open.
name: Densely Growing Evergreen Shrubs

desc:
This is the edge of an expanse of scrubland with thorn and other types of bush
and shrub hampering movement. To the north the scrubland thickens noticeably.
Further south the bushes and shrubs have tried to conquer the plains, but have
largely failed, leaving them dominated by short grass.
name: Edge of an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
This is the edge of an expanse of scrubland. Bushes and shrubs make travelling
in a straight line for any distance impossible. The scrubland thickens
considerably further north, while to the south the bushes disperse, eventually
vanishing into the gently sloping plains. More scrubland covers the land to the
east and the west.
name: Edge of an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
Bushes are scattered about, some of them tall, but their low density creates an
incomplete canopy, through which the sky can clearly be seen. The scrubland
stretches onward to the east and north but thins out to the west and south,
where grassy plains dominate the landscape.
name: Edge of an Expanse of Scrubland

desc:
Green grass grows tall beside the roadway, with some small bushes and shrubs
breaking up the uniformity. Eastward, the ground begins to slope gently upwards
and there are more bushes and shrubs, whilst to the north and south, the
grassland continues uninterrupted.
name: Fields beside the Greenway

desc:
Soft earth comprises the walls and floor of this hole, which is apparently home
to wild animals. Tufts of fur and a few small bones are scattered here and
there, but the den has an almost wholesome feeling about it, with no sign of
droppings or rotting matter to be found.
name: Inside an Earthy Hole

desc:
Clumps of dense bushes line a steep, grass-covered ledge, overlooking a
bush-covered valley to the northeast. Some small boulders and piles of rocks are
nearly hidden in the grass, providing ample cover for small animal burrows.
name: Dark Bushes

desc:
Clumps of tangled bushes dot the landscape here, amidst patches of tall grass.
To the west, the bushes grow gradually smaller until it's just rolling plains
running along the Greenway far to the west. The ground slopes downward to the
northeast into a dark valley.
name: Dark Brush

desc:
Fields of tall grass stretch out to the southeast, next to the passing Greenway.
To the east, the grasses are gradually replaced by groups of bushes and
transition into a dark brushland. Another patch of dense brushland stands across
the grass-covered road to the west.
name: Grasses by the Greenway

desc:
Bushes and grass cover the undergrowth, thick tree trunks towering above them
along a ledge to the east, overlooking a dark, bush-covered valley beyond. To
the southwest, the bushes grow thinner, revealing fields of grass and a glimpse
of the Greenway in the distance.
name: Overgrown Ledge

desc:
A thick tangle of bushes and tall grass grows around a pile of large boulders to
the north, probably fallen from the ridge to the northwest. The brush continues
on to the east, meeting a line of trees overlooking a steep ledge.
name: Tangled Rockfall

desc:
Tall grass surrounds the Greenway from this side, with a thick brush covering
the other side to the southwest. To the east, the grass is completely overtaken
by yet another swath of thick brush, bushes and smaller trees completely
obscuring the lands beyond.
name: Grasses by the Greenway

desc:
Below this tree-covered slope to the north, a secluded, bush-covered glen stands
between the thick brushland to the southeast and the Greenway far in the
distance to the northwest. Rows of strong trunks and thick branches form a
natural barrier on the edge of this slope.
name: Trees above a Bushy Glen

desc:
To the west, the Greenway can be seen running inside a narrow pass, meeting the
southern grasslands near a thick brush. To the east, similar brush covers the
landscape, obscuring terrain and wildlife from this vantage point. The grassy
ledge slopes even farther up to the north, ending at a steep ridge overlooking
the pass.
name: Grasses below a Ridge

desc:
This grass-covered ridge stands high above the pass to the northwest, with the
grass-covered Greenway running into it from the northern grasslands. To the
east, a vast brush covers the whole landscape. Carved into the ridge, a small
crevice leads down into the rock.
name: Ridge above the Greenway

desc:
The bushes next to the Greenway to the west are quite thick, obscuring vision
and providing cover from the road. On the other side of the road, there is a
little forested hill watching over the road as well. A few half-buried and
broken crates are almost hidden by the thick underbrush here.
name: Bushes next to a Road

desc:
To the west, the grass-covered Greenway runs next to a beech and birch forest
atop a small hill. On this side, there are only small bushes lining the road,
growing thicker further south and east. In a particularly dense clump next to
the road, the remains of an abandoned camp can be seen, well-hidden by the
vegetation.
name: Bushes along the Greenway

desc:
The grassy fields run along a bend in the Greenway to the northwest, meeting a
few bushes and small clumps of trees to the south which grow thicker as the
ground starts sloping downwards towards a brush-covered valley to the southeast.
name: Grasses by a Bend

desc:
Boulders are strewn among the bushes to the north, probably the result of a
small landslide. To the east, a narrow ledge leads closer to a steep canyon. The
canyon's edge zig-zags further south until turning slightly eastward. Through
the brush to the west, a forested hill can be seen far away, and the Greenway
meandering in front of it.
name: Small Clearing next to a Canyon

desc:
Thick brush covers the narrow westward passage between two ledges, leading
towards the Greenway running next to a forested hill. A steep canyon splits the
ground to the northeast, growing wider further north. Another bush-filled valley
can be seen beyond the ledge to the south.
name: Above a Canyon

desc:
To the northwest, a narrow, zig-zagging canyon splits the ground in two and
grows wider further north before disappearing into a very steep limestone cliff
far to the north. To the northeast, another small ledge runs along the canyon's
edge, above a narrow, grass-covered path.
name: Grassy Ledge

desc:
The jagged edge of a canyon runs along this grassy ledge, a solitary tree
growing on the opposite edge across the canyon to the west. To the east, a small
ledge runs parallel to the canyon, bushes and tufts of tall grass peering over
the edge.
name: Grassy Ledge

desc:
A steep limestone cliff blocks the passage north, roots sticking out of the
almost vertical wall. From the southwest, a deep canyon runs into the cliff
wall, forming a dark, narrow and extremely tall cavern inside the cliff. To the
east, the ground slopes up a bit in front of the cliff, towards a bush-covered
plateau.
name: Below a Cliff

desc:
To the west, the ground slopes down towards a deep canyon, separating the
bush-covered plains bordering the Greenway far to the west of this area. Passage
north is blocked by a vertical limestone cliff, some sections of it eroded and
fallen into this small valley.
name: Below a Cliff

desc:
The northern limestone cliff-face is almost completely vertical and looks
impossible to scale with conventional means. In some parts, the wall has eroded
quite a bit, leaving parts of the wall in piles of rubble among the grassy
ground here.
name: Below a Cliff

desc:
From atop this ledge, running parallel to a deep canyon to the west, the plains
beyond are quite visible, revealing the Greenway passing in front of a wooded
hill into a narrow pass far to the southwest. The bush-covered ground slopes
upwards to the northeast, towards a small hill.
name: Grassy Ledge

desc:
Below a steep limestone cliff to the north, a thick clump of bushes and small
trees grows on the edge of an even steeper canyon to the east. A big pile of
rubble has fallen out of the limestone cliff to the north, revealing a dark,
narrow crevice. Bigger boulders have rolled downhill into the bushes, blocking
movement farther into the brush.
name: Bushes below a Grassy Cliff

desc:
Rolling fields of grass line the edge of the grass-covered Greenway in the west
and stretch out in all other directions. Far to the east, a shelf rises up from
the ground, some small trees lining the edge and peering down into the endless
green.
name: Fields of Grass

desc:
Fields of tall grass cover the ground like a sea of green. To the east, a rocky
shelf angles upward, revealing a layered limestone wall. A few stunted trees
peer down from the edge of the shelf, their roots snaking downward and peering
out from inside the limestone.
name: Grass below a Limestone Shelf

desc:
The grass is thick and inviting, beckoning any weary traveller to rest in its
cool embrace. Wildflowers dance in the breeze, their bright petals a testament
to the fertility of the land. When the sun beats down upon the earth, its warmth
suffuses the air with a sense of peace and contentment.
name: Bushy Grassland

desc:
The rough plains stretch out, a barren landscape of yellow and brown grasses
that have been dried out by the sun. The ground is mostly bare, with only
patches of weeds and scraggly trees breaking up the monotony. A gentle breeze
rustles through the thin grass, the only sound in this desolate place.
name: Rough Plains

desc:
The plains here present a vast, open expanse of rolling hills and grassy fields.
The landscape is dotted with clusters of trees and wildflowers, adding splashes
of colour to the green vista. The air is filled with the sound of rustling grass
and the soft buzz of insects.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The landscape here is a wonder of nature, its rich soil nurturing an array of
plant life. Fields of grain wave in the wind, their golden stalks a testament to
the fertility of the land. Orchards of fruit trees offer a bounty of sweet
treats, while gardens bursting with colourful flowers add a touch of beauty to
the scene.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The low grassland stretches out, a flat terrain that rises to the east. Yellow
and orange wildflowers bloom in abundance, their petals bright against the sea
of thick green grass that carpets the ground. The air is filled with the sweet
aroma of the flowers, and a gentle breeze rustles the grass. In the distance,
the shadows of trees and hills can be spotted, marking the edge of this peaceful
meadow.
name: Low Grassland

desc:
The grassy field is a sea of green, undulating in the wind like the waves of an
ocean. Here and there, clusters of wildflowers add a touch of colour to the
landscape, their petals unfurling to soak up the sun's rays. The air is filled
with the sound of rustling grass and the soft buzz of insects.
name: Quiet Field

desc:
The field is quiet, with only the sound of the wind rustling through the grass.
The foliage is dense and green, with various animal paths, holes, and mounds
scattered throughout. It is a natural setting, undisturbed by any apparent human
presence. When the sun shines brightly overhead, it casts a warm glow over the
scene. It is a scene of simplicity and serenity, inviting further observation
and exploration.
name: Quiet Field

desc:
The grassy field is a tapestry of green, the tall blades of grass weaving
together to create a carpet of verdure. Wildflowers peek out from between the
grasses, their delicate petals adding a splash of colour to the scene. The sun
shines down upon the field, casting its golden glow over the landscape and
filling the air with the scent of growth and life.
name: Vast Grassland

desc:
The dry grasslands here form a scorched and parched landscape, the grass
withered and yellowed from the scorching sun and lack of moisture. The air is
hot and dry, and the earth is baked hard beneath the relentless heat, and mostly
bare.
name: Dry Grassland

desc:
The grassy field is a paradise of rolling hills and verdant valleys. The grass
is thick and lush, inviting the weary traveller to rest in its cool embrace.
Wildflowers dance in the breeze, their bright petals a testament to the field's
fertility.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
This low hill with steep and rocky banks stands apart from the verdant, green
hills to the west. The grass here is dark brown and withered, and in several
places it is gone altogether, leaving behind brown patches of chalky dust.
name: Withered Hill

desc:
This chamber is lined with blocks of grey stone and appears to have been hastily
built, judging from the great cracks that have formed between adjacent blocks.
Scattered about the floor are the remains of several human skeletons along with
bits of armour and weapons. None of the refuse appears to be of use. Perhaps
this place was looted some time in the distant past.
name: Burial Chamber

desc:
This small area houses a stone table, upon which lie the remains of some fallen
warrior of the distant past. The dry air of this place has caused him to mummify
-- taut skin stretches tightly over his skeletal frame. The rotted remnants of a
white robe still cling to the body, and his hands are folded over his chest.
There is a carving of some sort high on the southern wall, still clearly legible
after hundreds of years.
name: Inner Chamber

desc:
From atop this limestone shelf, the Greenway can be seen far to the west,
snaking through the endless grass fields until meeting a forested hill to the
southwest. To the south, the grassy fields slope downward into a brush- filled
valley.
name: Grass-covered Shelf

desc:
A rocky, grass-covered limestone shelf slopes up to the west, with a few small
trees lining the edges. A few boulders peer out from among the tall grass near
the foot of the shelf, next to the tips of tree roots piercing through the
stone.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
To the south, the grass-lined cliff's edge offers a good view over the valley.
Narrow, brush-covered canyons and crevices run back and forth in the slowly
downward-sloping valley, vegetation growing ever thicker and thicker farther
south.
name: Grass-covered Cliff

desc:
Fields of lush, tall grass run between a limestone shelf to the north and a
steep drop to the south, overlooking a valley filled with narrow canyons,
zig-zagging between stretches of thick brush and patches of grassy fields.
name: Grass-covered Cliff

desc:
In the distance to the northwest, the Greenway can be seen cutting through the
grassy fields. A steeper limestone shelf peers out from the flat grassland to
the northwest, a few stray boulders lining its foot. To the south, the rolling
green fields suddenly stop at a steep cliff's edge.
name: Grass-covered Cliff

desc:
The lush green grasslands stretch out in all directions, with only the Greenway
lazily meandering through them to the west. Beyond a sudden drop to the south,
the ground starts sloping downward towards some jagged, bush-lined rock
formations.
name: Grasses above a Drop

desc:
The narrow crevice in the limestone slopes downward to the north, leading to
quite a steep drop. The uneven walls are damp, with small patches of lichen
growing out of crevices and hanging down from the ceiling. To the south, the
crevice widens a bit, revealing some dense brush outside.
name: Narrow Limestone Crevice

desc:
The crevice slopes upward to the south, a pile of rubble providing a somewhat
stable surface to climb upon. To the east, the crevice opens up into a huge
cavern. A large round boulder blocks immediate passage to the cavern but grooves
worn into the cavern floor beside it hint that it can probably be moved quite
easily.
name: Bottom of a Limestone Crevice

desc:
The tunnel's limestone walls are pale and smooth, their surface worn down by the
steady flow of ancient water. Subtle ripples and grooves trace the rock, giving
the passage an organic, flowing appearance. The air is cool and damp, carrying
the faint scent of wet stone.
name: Smooth Corridor

desc:
The tunnel opens into a broader space, the limestone walls displaying natural
columns where mineral-rich water has solidified over centuries. Stalactites and
stalagmites are beginning to meet, forming delicate pillars in places. The sound
of dripping water echoes softly, filling the space with a sense of timeless
stillness.
name: Wet Tunnel

desc:
A narrow stretch of thick bushes grows between a small brush-covered ledge to
the north and another steep drop into a valley to the southeast. The Greenway
can be seen beyond the bushes to the west, turning a corner around a wooded hill
beyond.
name: Bush-covered Ledge

desc:
This steep ledge is overlooking a small bushy grove to the southwest, with grass
and small bushes covering its uneven surface. The edges look sharp and uneven,
eroded by wind and rainfall over the years. To the east, there's a steep
limestone wall, overgrown with vines.
name: Narrow Ledge

desc:
A narrow passageway opens to reveal a small crevice in the sandstone, where the
sunlight filters through cracks above. The walls here are smooth but pocked with
small indentations, worn down over time. Loose sand collects in the corners,
stirred occasionally by the wind wafting in from above.
name: Sunlit Passage

desc:
The ground slopes down towards the southeast, away from the Greenway, into a
small secluded glen. Thick bushes with a layer of tall grass underneath line the
bottom of this glen, bordered by a steep, layered limestone cliff to the north.
name: Bushy Glen

desc:
This corner of the glen is surrounded by steep limestone ledges to the north and
east, overlooking layers of bushes and grass that cover the ground around here.
On the opposite side of this secluded valley, a high rocky ridge stands watch
over the Greenway.
name: Bushy Glen

desc:
In the southeastern corner of this secluded glen, small trees seem to be taking
over the otherwise bush-covered valley floor. As the ground slopes up to the
south, the trees grow even taller and wider in trunk and bough before
practically becoming a thick wall of trunks and tree limbs on the glen's edge.
name: Bushy Glen

desc:
Nestled between a high rocky ridge to the west and a huge rockfall to the south,
this corner of the bush-covered glen offers quite a lot of cover from the
elements. Beyond the piles of boulders of the rockfall, some larger trees can be
seen, meeting the valley's edge to the southeast.
name: Bushy Glen below a Ridge

desc:
Treetrunks and thick bushes grow out from beneath a massive rockfall, boulders
and smaller rocks strewn all about the undergrowth. To the northwest, a rocky
ridge rises up and sits between the thick brushland to the southeast and the
Greenway to the northwest.
name: Wooded Rockfall

desc:
Below a steep ledge to the west, a narrow path snakes down towards the valley
towards the northeast, zig-zagging between boulders and bushes. Another steep
drop leads into a deep crevice to the east, its floor covered with even thicker
bushes.
name: Bushy Slope

desc:
The hillside continues to spiral downward to the northeast, meeting the dark,
bush-covered floor below. A sharp ledge stands to the southwest, overlooking the
hillside, patches of tall grass peering down from up above. An uneven path leads
uphill to the south, away from the valley.
name: Hillside below a Ledge

desc:
The steep ledge to the west makes a sharp turn to the southwest, creating a
little concealed nook. A somewhat large and round boulder seems to have made
this into its nest, resting against the ledge as if having taken a breather
before continuing down into the valley to the northeast.
name: Concealed Nook

desc:
To the east, the hillside seems to abruptly drop down into the low, bush-covered
valley. A somewhat level path runs along the bottom of a tree-covered ledge to
the west, meandering between boulders and bushes of various sizes.
name: Bushy Drop

desc:
Part of the western hillside has eroded away, leaving behind a small gap between
a higher ledge to the north and a wood-covered hilltop to the southwest
overlooking this valley. Beyond the gap lies another bush-covered valley,
leading towards the Greenway far to the west.
name: Gap in the Hills

desc:
The rocky hillside slopes towards the southeast, its uneven terrain covered with
thick vegetation. Tall grass and a thick bushes bushes form a canopy over the
rocks and boulders that litter the landscape. The hillside looks to be slightly
more even to the west, running below a tree-covered ledge.
name: Uneven Hillside

desc:
Short, narrow walls, and a drooping, root-covered ceiling make this dirt-carved
cave extremely cramped. The confined space also accentuates the pungent smell of
damp fur, making the musty odour the most prominent feature of this dirty abode.
name: Cramped Cave

desc:
The uneven hillside slopes down into a bushy valley to the southeast, but this
particular ledge is still high enough to provide a view over the northwestern
terrain. A steep canyon can be seen far to the northwest, surrounded by grassy
ledges.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
The uneven hillside angles downward to the south, towards a dark, bush-covered
valley. The vegetation covering the hillside is thick, with large, thorny bushes
providing a dark canopy above. There seems to be a sudden drop directly south of
here.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
This seems to be a local high point as the ground slopes downwards in all
directions, covered with thick bushes and patches of tough grass. The dark
valley stretching far to the south is evenly covered with the same kind of
thorny brush.
name: Bushy Hill

desc:
Below a ledge to the west, the tip of a deep canyon can be seen running north
and meeting a large cliff. There's another higher ledge to the south, thick and
thorny bushes lining the edge, leading towards the little hilltop to the
southeast.
name: Bushy Ledge

desc:
Small thorny bushes dot the landscape here, tufts of tall grass and boulders
among them. To the west, a small ledge looks over a steep drop towards a deep
canyon. Farther south, the bushes grow more dense and the ground seems to angle
upwards a bit.
name: Bushy Ledge

desc:
The uneven valley floor here is covered with bushes, tufts of grass and piles of
rocks, having settled here after tumbling down from above. To the south, the
ground seems to slope even further down into a round basin, its floor completely
covered with rockfall.
name: Bottom of a Valley

desc:
A particularly large and round boulder sits right in the middle of the valley
floor here, surrounded by bushes and clumps of tall grass. A narrow, barely
visible footpath seems to lead away from the boulder towards the upward slope to
the southwest. Some weather-worn scratches and markings can be barely seen on
the surface of the boulder.
name: Peculiar Boulder

desc:
Steep cliffs stretch upward to the west, overlooking this somewhat secluded part
of the valley. Most of the ground here is evenly covered with bushes and grass,
some of it so thick that it makes any passage through impossible.
name: Dense Brushland

desc:
The ground is covered with bushes and grass, boulders and rubble littered among
them, doubtlessly fallen from atop the nigh-vertical cliffside to the north.
Animal droppings and splinters of bone lie half-hidden in the grass, hinting at
some somewhat recent animal activity in the area.
name: Bottom of a Cliff

desc:
This lower part of the valley floor is almost circular in shape, hinting there
was a sinkhole here ages ago. Now, it has almost completely filled in with
rockfall from above. From between the fallen boulders, vines and bushes reach up
towards the sky.
name: Collapsed Sinkhole

desc:
Boulders large and small line the walls of this cave of packed dirt and stone. A
few roots peak out from the ceiling, low enough to cause taller adventurers to
duck. A small opening in the western wall leads out into the dark brush beyond.
name: Boulder-Strewn Cave

desc:
This walls of this large cave are marked with deep gouges, evidently made by
some massive claws. The floor is dirt, but packed hard as stone by the tread of
hefty paws. A deep depression at the end of the cavern is apparently where some
hulking beasts have made their bed.
name: Spacious Bear Den

desc:
This narrow tunnel angles downward to the south. A barely noticeable footpath
has been beaten down into the tunnel floor. A few spots of earth are scorched,
perhaps from fires or a hot lantern. To the west, the tunnel opens up into a
wide field.
name: Very Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel comes to an abrupt end here, with dark walls closing in from all
