directions save the north. The faint footpath visible in the dirt floor ends
abruptly toward the back of the tunnel, next to some smooth rock. Very little
dust covers that area of the tunnel.
name: End of a Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The walls and ceiling of the tunnel are rough and uneven, with cracks of various
sizes running through them at odd angles. The tunnel ends abruptly to the north
with a sharp upward turn. A wooden ladder is mounted to the wall, providing
access to the opening. To the south, the tunnel leads farther into the rock.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A wooden walkway connects the northern and southern tunnels, with another side
passage branching off to the east. Various smaller cracks and crevices lead to
even narrower crevices, all snaking back into the main tunnels, creating quite a
maze.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
After a few twists and turns, the tunnel ends at a cave-in, blocking further
passage to the east. A few broken crates and the remains of a smashed barrel lie
in a pile in the middle of the cave. Beyond a wide crack in the cave wall to the
south, a narrow side tunnel leads farther into the rock.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
An intersection of tunnels sits on a slight slope, with larger passages leading
up to the northern caves, another larger passage branching off to the east and
an even narrower tunnel leading south and turning sharply to the west. A barely
burning torch is mounted into a wall sconce on the western cavern wall,
providing dim light.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A few wooden support beams are propped up to hold the northeastern corner of the
cave from collapsing. A rough wooden walkway provides a somewhat more stable
surface to walk on, leading south along the main tunnel. To the west, a dimly
lit cave can be seen in the distance.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The passage south is blocked by a sturdy-looking wooden wall, with a large
reinforced door in the middle. Wooden supports line the walls to the east and
south, along with a wooden walkway leading into the dark tunnel to the north.
name: End of the Tunnel

desc:
A roughly rectangular room is built at the end of the tunnel here, worn boards
mounted between a line of support beams that probably keep the whole tunnel from
collapsing. A large table sits in the middle of the room, covered in a pile of
old maps, mostly depicting the surrounding area. More maps and documents cover
the walls. In the corner, a large trapdoor is built into the floor.
name: Dimly-lit Room

desc:
A dilapidated wooden staircase leads from the large trapdoor in the ceiling to
the uneven floor of the cellar. Piles of crates and large barrels line the walls
here. A somewhat fresh-looking trail of dirt leads from the staircase to the
northern wall.
name: Dark Storage Cellar

desc:
Wooden boards and beams of various sizes have been nailed together to form a
surprisingly sturdy-looking wall, only a single crude door in the middle of it
blocking passage south. The bottom of the barricade looks half-rotten, as a
shallow puddle of water seems to have collected here from the dampness of the
surrounding caves.
name: Barricaded Tunnel

desc:
The low tunnel turns a sharp corner to the east and ends abruptly at a wooden
construction of sorts. To the north, the tunnel's ceiling grows higher and
higher, opening up into the wide canyon. The ground here is damp, with shallow
puddles of water lining the walls.
name: Damp Tunnel

desc:
The worn and uneven walls of the tunnel are moist, with liquid dripping along
the wall and collecting into small puddles on the ground. The tunnel opens up to
the north, revealing a large canyon with steep limestone walls to either side.
To the south, the tunnel grows darker and narrower.
name: Moist Tunnel

desc:
The flat floor of the canyon is riddled with puddles of water. The walls are
steep and quite smooth, with tiny streams of water dripping down along cracks
and crevices in the surface. A huge pile of worn ropes sits in a mess along the
western wall, next to a harness and some empty crates.
name: In a Narrow Canyon

desc:
A few worn wooden blocks are placed around the remains of a campfire next to the
eastern wall of the canyon, right below a small outcropping that provides some
cover from above. A few weather-worn sleeping bags are placed next to the
campfire. To the north, the canyon seems to grow a bit narrower.
name: At the Bottom of the Canyon

desc:
The walls of the canyon are pretty close to each other now, with only about six
feet separating the two. To the north, the walls meet completely at a narrow
crevice, leading into a dark limestone cavern. To the south, there seems to be a
small camping spot below an outcropping in the eastern wall.
name: At the Entrance of a Cave

desc:
The cavern walls are quite smooth and damp, with tiny streams of water running
along the ground, leaving behind deep crevices and grooves. To the north, the
ground seems to slope upward slightly until ending abruptly at a large stone
slab blocking further passage. A wide crack to the south opens up into a narrow
canyon.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
A huge set of stone stairs has been carved into the soft limestone in the
southern end of the cavern, leading down toward an opening in the cavern's
floor. To the north, the cavern grows a lot wider and higher, with huge
stalagmites and stalactites forming a semblance of an enormous maw.
name: Dark Limestone Tunnel

desc:
Rock formations of various sizes fill this part of the cavern, with huge
stalagmites being especially dominant like some sort of an underground rocky
forest of sorts. A quite smooth walkway seems to be worn into the ground,
leading towards the northwestern corner of the cavern. A narrow tunnel branches
off from the cavern to the south.
name: Forest of Stalagmites

desc:
The end of a large stalagmite seems to have been completely severed from its
trunk, lying sideways within a shallow puddle on the ground. The cavern seems to
lead into a narrower section to the southwest and the ground hints at a slight
upward slope in that direction.
name: Broken Stalagmite

desc:
Nestled between a pair of huge stalagmites, a shallow puddle of clear water
covers the floor here, fed by a number of tiny streams of water dripping down
along the walls and rock formations. The cavern ceiling is too high to be seen,
with only a few sharp ends of stalactites hinting at its position.
name: Puddle among the Stalagmites

desc:
A particularly large pillar of stone stands here, seemingly holding up the whole
cavern, surrounded by smaller stalagmites and stalactites on either side. Tiny
streams of water make their way around the rock formations, forming small
puddles on the ground.
name: Damp Limestone Pillar

desc:
The northwestern corner of the huge cavern ends with a narrow crevice, almost
hidden behind a large round boulder standing neatly on the floor. To the
southeast, large stalagmites jut out from the uneven cavern floor, concealing
the rest of the cavern.
name: Corner of a Huge Cavern

desc:
A somewhat even pathway meanders between huge stalagmites extending out from the
otherwise uneven ground here. The cavern ceiling is quite high above, with only
the pointy ends of some huge stalactites peering out from the darkness.
name: Huge Limestone Cavern

desc:
A narrow, natural-looking crevice leads into a wider tunnel inside the ridge.
Jagged rocks jut out from the walls at odd angles. At the bottom of the tunnel,
the ground is moist, with rotting remains of small animals scattered around a
small pool of water.
name: Jagged Crevice

desc:
The tunnel opens into a wider space, the floor littered with large, jagged rocks
that have fallen from the uneven sandstone ceiling. The walls are less smooth
here, marked by rough edges and deep fissures where time has torn the stone
apart. A faint scent of earth and rock fills the air.
name: Rocky Threshold

desc:
The floor of the tunnel gradually slopes downward to the south, the walls
tapering as the passage narrows. The sandstone is worn in places, with patches
of rough texture contrasting against smoother sections. Small stones and debris
shift underfoot with each step.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
A small square-shaped room is dug into the rock here, hidden behind the
reinforced wall to the north. Various wooden crates, musty sacks and barrels are
stacked against the walls. A flickering torch sits in a wall sconce, providing
dim light.
name: Hidden Cache

desc:
Polished white bones cover every inch of this chamber. Each bone gleams, having
been sucked clean of every vestige of flesh and muscle. To the east, a dark pool
splashes against the shore, washing the bones with every shift of current.
name: Massive Pile of Bones
